perturbation.original_text: Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Business as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Business or the Acquired Assets; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Business and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Business as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers. Notwithstanding the preceding, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Business or the Acquired Assets, can be disclosed at any time; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Business and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 1.1. The original text stated that Sellers shall not disclose any information pertaining to the customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures. The modified version allows for the disclosure of vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures, contradicting the earlier statement about not disclosing such information. This creates uncertainty regarding what information Sellers are prohibited from disclosing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Non-Disclosure . Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Business as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers. Notwithstanding the preceding, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Business or the Acquired Assets, can be disclosed at any time; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Business and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction in Section 1.1 as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The original text states that Sellers shall not disclose information pertaining to customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures. The modified version allows for the disclosure of vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures, contradicting the earlier statement about not disclosing such information, creating a strong and direct conflict within the document. This is also a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction based on the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply only for a one-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply for the full three-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business.
perturbation.explanation: The change made in this section introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the restriction period applied to successors of the seller. The original text states the restrictions apply only for a one-year period, while the changed text states the restrictions apply for the full three-year period. This contradiction creates confusion and uncertainty regarding the duration of non-competition restrictions for successors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Non-Competition . For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply for the full three-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business. Because Sellers’ Business is nationwide, Sellers agree that they will not so compete anywhere in the United States (the “ Territory ”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sellers may, during the 60-day period following the Effective Date, provide services in the Business to those customers delineated on Schedule 1.02(c) of the Purchase Agreement, subject to and in accordance with Section 5.08 of the Purchase Agreement; provided such services shall be provided solely pursuant to the customer contracts listed on such Schedule 1.02(c) and the amount of such service shall not be materially greater than that provided to such customers by Sellers during the 60-day period prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the non-competition restrictions applicable to successors of the seller from a one-year period to a full three-year period. The contradicted text states that the restrictions apply for the full three-year period to the successors of the seller. The original text contradicts the contradicted text, where as the changed text aligns. This means that there is a contradiction between the Original Text and the Changed Text and the question is whether the Changed Text makes a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. Since the changed text now agrees with the contradicted text there is no contradiction. Therefore the answer is NO.

perturbation.original_text: Any breach of any of the covenants set forth in this Article 1 would result in irreparable damage to the Buyer. Consequently, and without limiting other remedies which may exist for a breach of this Agreement, the Buyer will have the right to enforce such covenants by obtaining a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction restraining any violation hereof, pending or following a trial on the merits, without posting any bond and in addition to all other remedies available to the Buyer at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Any breach of any of the covenants set forth in this Article 1 would result in irreparable damage to the Buyer. Consequently, and without limiting other remedies which may exist for a breach of this Agreement, the Buyer will have the right to enforce such covenants by obtaining a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction restraining any violation hereof, pending or following a trial on the merits, after posting a bond, and in addition to all other remedies available to the Buyer at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: This changes Section 1.4, regarding remedies, to contradict itself. The original version explicitly states the Buyer can obtain injunctive relief without posting a bond. The modification requires the Buyer to post a bond, thus contradicting the earlier statement within the same section and creating uncertainty about the conditions required to obtain an injunction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Remedies . Any breach of any of the covenants set forth in this Article 1 would result in irreparable damage to the Buyer. Consequently, and without limiting other remedies which may exist for a breach of this Agreement, the Buyer will have the right to enforce such covenants by obtaining a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction restraining any violation hereof, pending or following a trial on the merits, after posting a bond, and in addition to all other remedies available to the Buyer at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 1.4 to state that the Buyer must post a bond to obtain injunctive relief, while the original text of 1.4 states the Buyer can obtain such relief without posting a bond. The Contradicted Text is the exact same text as the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction within the contract regarding whether a bond is required, which is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Sellers acknowledge and agree that the covenants contained in this Agreement are reasonable in all respects, including without limitation their duration and geographic scope. Sellers expressly waive any defenses as to the reasonableness of such covenants in any action between the parties to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sellers acknowledge and agree that the covenants contained in this Agreement are reasonable in all respects, including without limitation their duration and geographic scope. Sellers expressly retain the right to challenge the reasonableness of such covenants in any action between the parties to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction in Section 1.5. The original text has the Sellers waiving any defenses regarding the reasonableness of the covenants. The changed text has the Sellers retaining the right to challenge the reasonableness. This contradiction immediately creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Reasonableness of Covenants . Sellers acknowledge and agree that the covenants contained in this Agreement are reasonable in all respects, including without limitation their duration and geographic scope. Sellers expressly retain the right to challenge the reasonableness of such covenants in any action between the parties to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text (which are actually the same text). Originally, the sellers waive any defenses to the reasonableness of the covenants. The changed text states that the sellers retain the right to challenge the reasonableness of the covenants. These are mutually exclusive statements creating a clear in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the seller's rights.

perturbation.original_text: “Control” (including the terms “Controlled by” and “under common Control with”) means, as used with respect to any Person, possession, directly or indirectly or as a trustee or executor, of power to direct or cause the direction of management or policies of such Person (whether through ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by agreement or otherwise).
perturbation.changed_text: “Control” (including the terms “Controlled by” and “under common Control with”) means, as used with respect to any Person, possession, directly or indirectly or as a trustee or executor, of power to direct or cause the direction of management or policies of such Person (whether through ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by agreement or otherwise). Notwithstanding the forgoing, Control can only be established through direct ownership of more than 50% of voting securities.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a contradiction to the definition of 'Control' in Section 1.6(b). The original definition allows for control to be established through various means, including agreement or otherwise. The added sentence restricts control solely to direct ownership of more than 50% of voting securities, contradicting the original broad definition. This could lead to disputes over whether control exists in situations where there is influence without majority ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) “Control” (including the terms “Controlled by” and “under common Control with”) means, as used with respect to any Person, possession, directly or indirectly or as a trustee or executor, of power to direct or cause the direction of management or policies of such Person (whether through ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by agreement or otherwise). Notwithstanding the forgoing, Control can only be established through direct ownership of more than 50% of voting securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction within the definition of 'Control'. The original definition allows for control through various means (agreement, etc.), while the added sentence restricts it solely to direct ownership of more than 50% of voting securities. This directly conflicts with the original broad definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text and contradicted text are in the same location.
